article_title: systemd 261 Ships Final With a Software-TPM Fallback and Promotes sysupdate Out of Experimental Status

content_preview.title: systemd 261 Ships Final With a Software-TPM Fallback and Promotes sysupdate Out of Experimental Status
content_preview.summary: The final systemd 261 release adds systemd-tpm2-swtpm, a software TPM fallback for hardware without a TPM chip, and graduates systemd-sysupdate and a new networkd DHCP-relay backend.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The systemd project has published the final release of systemd 261, tagged [v261](https://github.com/systemd/systemd/releases/tag/v261) on 19 June 2026. Beyond the headline features that arrived with the first release candidate, the shipping version adds a service that runs a software-based Trusted Platform Module on systems without a hardware TPM, moves the operating-system image updater out of experimental status, and gives the network manager a new DHCP relay backend.

The Machin...

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (503 words, within the 400-1200 range). Clean Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know framing. Correctly positions itself as a force-follow-up to the June 5 RC article: the new shipping-release items (software-TPM fallback, sysupdate graduation, DHCP-relay backend) are foregrounded, while the RC features (sysinstall, IMDS, LUO/KHO) are explicitly labeled 'carried over from the release candidate.'
editor_notes.source_verification: Read all three snapshots from disk (status 200 each). source-1.html.gz (systemd v261 NEWS, primary): verified verbatim quotes for systemd-tpm2-swtpm.service ('A new service systemd-tpm2-swtpm.service has been added that can run the IBM "swtpm" as a software TPM, for use as an (optional) automatic fallback for systems that lack a physical TPM but where TPM functionality should be made available nonetheless.'), systemd-sysupdate ('systemd-sysupdate is now installed in /usr/bin/ alongside the other user-facing tools, as it is no longer considered experimental.'), sd-dhcp-relay ('systemd-networkd gained a new sd-dhcp-relay backend for DHCP relay agent support.'), plus the carried-over sysinstall, IMDS, and LUO/KHO quotes — all exact. DHCP-relay config specifics confirmed: deprecated [DHCPServer] settings BindToInterface=, RelayTarget=, RelayAgentCircuitId=, RelayAgentRemoteId=, replaced by DHCPRelay= in [Network] and a new [DHCPRelay] section. swtpm kernel-cmdline gating (systemd.tpm2_software_fallback=) confirmed in NEWS. source-0.html.gz (GitHub release tag v261): confirmed the v261 tag dated 2026-06-19 (datetime 2026-06-19T18:12:16Z), matching the article's '19 June 2026.' source-2.html.gz (Help Net Security, 2026/06/22): confirmed both facts attributed to it — swtpm software fallback gated behind a kernel command line option, and sysupdate left experimental status and moved to /usr/bin/.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every component name, the tag date, and the DHCP-relay config specifics trace to the primary NEWS file / GitHub release tag per Rule 9. The two facts dual-attributed to Help Net Security are confirmed in that snapshot. No fabricated specifics, numbers, or quotes. Cross-reference link (/article/2026-06/05-systemd-261-reaches-...) resolves to a real published article on main.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and technical throughout. No sensationalism, no AI self-reference. The 'What We Don't Know' section appropriately scopes the adoption/timeline limits of the release notes.
editor_notes.originality: Genuine follow-up to the June 5 systemd 261-rc1 article. The prior RC article contains none of the three new items (swtpm, sysupdate graduation, DHCP-relay backend) — confirmed by grep — so there is no duplication; this covers what is new in the stable release.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: APPROVE. All integrity checks pass; signature valid. All three sources read from snapshot; every cited quote is verbatim from the NEWS file and every specific (component names, 19 June 2026 tag date, DHCP-relay config) traces to the primary source per Rule 9. Help-Net-Security-attributed facts confirmed. Cross-reference resolves and framing correctly centers on the stable release vs the RC. All source domains (github.com, raw.githubusercontent.com, helpnetsecurity.com) are on the allowlist.
